feature.name: Pointer Lock on Android
feature.creator: abdoeed@google.com
feature.summary: Provides access to raw mouse movement by locking the target of mouse events to a single element and hiding the mouse cursor.

intake.finding: ChromeStatus record (created 2025-12-02 by abdoeed@google.com). Provides access to raw mouse movement by locking the target of mouse events to a single element and hiding the mouse cursor. Category: User input. Blink component: Blink>Input>PointerLock. Stars: 82.
intake.limitations: ChromeStatus record fields only; intent thread content not directly accessible.

incubation.finding: Feature targets web feature 'pointer-lock'. This is an official WebDX feature. Problem definition depends on the spec/explainer which was not directly accessible.
incubation.limitations: No public explainer or incubation discussion accessible.

prototype.finding: No public evidence of prototype-stage friction logs, API ergonomics testing, or framework integration.

developerTrials.finding: No developer trial was conducted or required for this feature.

wideReview.finding: Feature maps to official WebDX feature 'pointer-lock'. Cross-engine positions not verified in this report.

adoption.finding: The WebDX/WebFeature counter 'PointerLock' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 52) measured 0.0565% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2024-06-10 with 579 data points. Mapping confidence: declared-family.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures the PointerLock family/property, not this specific feature. Does not isolate the Chrome 144 increment.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.

developerValue.finding: Addresses: Provides access to raw mouse movement by locking the target of mouse events to a single element and hiding the mouse cursor.

endUserImpact.finding: End-user impact not analyzed in this report.
